channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iran Holds State Funeral for Assassinated Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei
item.description: Iran is holding extensive funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the assassinated supreme leader, following his killing in a US-Israeli strike. The events, spanning six days, are expected to d...

item.title: Maasai Mara University Offers 21 Teaching and Administrative Roles
item.description: Maasai Mara University, a public institution chartered in 2013, has announced the availability of 21 job vacancies across two key divisions: Academic and Student Affairs, and Administration, Finance, ...

item.title: Regina Roughriders secure 27-22 win after late defensive stand against Redblacks
item.description: The Regina Roughriders extended their winning streak with a hard-fought 27-22 victory over the Ottawa Redblacks, showcasing resilience in the final minutes of the game. Despite a late rally by the Red...

item.title: Journalist Abducted Over Report On CBN Deputy Governor
item.description: A journalist from Secrets Reporters, Stanley Ugagbe, has gone missing after being allegedly abducted by security operatives linked to the CBN Deputy Governor, Emem Nnanna Etuk Usoro. The publisher of ...

item.title: BJP Accuses Government of Conspiracy to Undermine Democracy
item.description: The article discusses allegations by the BJP against the government of a 'dangerous conspiracy' aimed at undermining democratic processes. The BJP claims the government is orchestrating a plot to erod...

item.title: South Africans' Reliance on Cash Savings Despite Formal Banking Access
item.description: Nearly half of South Africans continue to store savings in cash at home despite expanded access to formal banking services, according to FinMark Trust and World Bank data. This trend highlights persis...

item.title: Ombudsman Dismisses Mao Aplasca Following Senate Shooting Incident
item.description: Ombudsman Jesus Crispin Remulla announced the dismissal of Mao Aplasca from his post as Acting Sergeant at Arms of the Senate and Commission on Appointments following a controversial incident. Aplasca...

item.title: Saskatchewan Roughriders' defense holds off Ottawa Redblacks in close game
item.description: The Saskatchewan Roughriders extended their winning streak with a 27-22 victory over the winless Ottawa Redblacks, showcasing their defensive resilience. Despite the Redblacks' efforts to mount a late...

item.title: Six Men Arrested for Bidding on Construction Projects in Palisades Fire Zone Without Licenses
item.description: Six men have been arrested for allegedly bidding on construction projects in the fire-ravaged Palisades neighborhood of Pacific Palisades without proper contractor licenses, according to Los Angeles C...

item.title: Tash Appears at UDA Rally, Sparks Political Speculation
item.description: Kenyan businessman Tash, known for his late wife Betty Bayo's legacy, has sparked online debates after appearing at a United Democratic Alliance (UDA) rally in Nyandarua County. The event, held in Ol ...

item.title: Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial: Legal Team Overview
item.description: The article details the legal team representing Vice President Sara Duterte during her impeachment trial. Key members include Michael Wesley Poa, the spokesperson, and Roberto Batungbacal, a senior pa...

item.title: Resilience in the Face of Adversity: The Story of Sea Cottage and South Africa's Journey
item.description: The article recounts the remarkable story of Sea Cottage, a racehorse shot in the rump by gangsters in 1966, which inspired a metaphor for South Africa's resilience. Despite the bullet lodged in his b...

item.title: Americans Opt for Domestic Travel This Summer Amid Rising Costs
item.description: The article discusses a growing trend of Americans prioritizing domestic travel over international vacations during the summer of 2024, driven by rising costs of airfare, gasoline, and overall travel ...

item.title: Heche’s driver passes away in Kigoma, burial scheduled in Tarime
item.description: The driver of the late Tanzanian gospel artist Ringtone Apoko, popularly known as Heche, has died in Kigoma region in western Tanzania. According to the report, the driver passed away under circumstan...

item.title: July 4th Market Closure Dates and Impact on Trading
item.description: The stock market will be closed on July 4th, 2026, for the Independence Day holiday, affecting trading schedules and investor strategies. This closure is part of the standard holiday observance in the...

item.title: Punjab Congress faction demands leadership change amid internal conflicts
item.description: The article discusses internal divisions within the Punjab Congress party, where leaders including Charanjit Singh Channi and Sukhjinder Singh Randhawa are pushing for a leadership reshuffle. Channi's...

item.title: Messi's 20th World Cup goal helps Argentina edge out Cape Verde in dramatic extra-time clash
item.description: Lionel Messi reached another historic milestone by scoring his 20th FIFA World Cup goal as defending champions Argentina survived a spirited Cape Verde fightback to claim a dramatic 3-2 extra-time vic...

item.title: Non-surgical Treatments for Saggy Skin: Expert Insights on Effective and Ineffective Options
item.description: The article explores the challenges of saggy skin following weight loss and examines non-surgical methods to address this issue. It highlights the importance of lifestyle adjustments, including diet a...

item.title: A Remote House in Iceland's Vestman Islands and Its Surprising History
item.description: The article discusses a house on Elliðaey Island, part of the Vestman archipelago in Iceland, often mistaken for the 'most solitary house on Earth.' Built by hunters in the 1950s to study and hunt ful...

item.title: Tanzania's Political Tensions: Opposition and Government Clash Over Policies
item.description: Tanzania's opposition leader Tundu Lissu has intensified criticisms against the government, prompting swift rebuttals from officials defending President Samia Suluhu Hassan's administration. The polit...
